claim: Research becomes durable product leverage only after it is normalized into doctrine and packets.
mechanism: Broad analysis documents are useful for human understanding, but evaluators and retrieval systems usually depend on smaller, structured artifacts. Converting research into doctrines and packets preserves the mechanism and boundary in a reusable form, which allows later runs to retrieve exactly the lesson they need instead of rereading an entire report.
boundary: Normalization should not erase nuance. Some frontier findings should remain broad notes until the mechanism is clearer.
